examples.output: Recommendation: Use JRN probe to rank joins before feature engineering (6.5x speedup vs greedy on rel-f1 with 13 joins)
Recommendation: Universal JRN ranking is viable (same-entity rho=0.82-0.87); compute JRN once and reuse across tasks
Recommendation: Compute per-task JRN rankings (cross-entity rho=0.24 to -0.70); universal rankings fail across entity boundaries
Recommendation: Use log-linear compounding model to predict chain JRN from individual JRN values (R2=0.83 on GBM, Spearman=0.91)

examples.input: Scenario: Need to understand JRN signal source
examples.output: Recommendation: Feature quality dominates structural signal; FK-shuffling shows structural component is significant but secondary (Cohen's d=0.63-0.69)

examples.input: Scenario: Tight compute budget for JRN estimation
examples.output: Recommendation: Use cheapest LightGBM config (25 trees, depth 3, 10% subsample): achieves rho>0.95 with 15x speedup

examples.input: Scenario: Choosing between MLP and GBM probes
examples.output: Recommendation: Prefer GBM probes for compounding analysis (R2=0.83 vs 0.12 for MLP on log-linear model)
Recommendation: JRN probe achieves 96-99% of greedy performance at 5-11% of the cost; always preferable for J>1 joins
Recommendation: No direct threshold experiment was run; treat JRN near 1.0 as uncertain signal. JRN>1.1 likely informative, JRN<0.9 likely uninformative
Recommendation: Start with cheapest GBM config (25 trees, 10% data) for initial JRN rankings, validate with full budget if rankings seem unexpected

examples.input: Claim 1: JRN as valid per-join metric
examples.output: Grade: A (strong). Probe rho>0.9 (exp_id4_it5), structural signal significant p=0.0001 d=0.69 (rel-f1), p=0.024 d=0.63 (rel-stack)

examples.input: Claim 2: Threshold behavior (non-monotonic variance near JRN~1)
examples.output: Grade: D (absent). No direct aggregation-strategy variance experiment was run. Cannot evaluate peak near JRN=1.
Grade: B (strong). 5.5-18x speedup vs greedy, 96-99% performance. Single dataset (rel-f1).
Grade: C (moderate). Log-linear model R2=0.83 on GBM (rel-f1), Spearman=0.68 on rel-stack. Naive multiplicative fails (negative R2). Partially supported: signal compounds but not multiplicatively.
Grade: C (moderate). JRN achieves 96-99% of greedy at 5-11% cost. Does NOT outperform on raw performance. Cost-adjusted win.
Grade: B (moderate). Structural component significant but secondary (dominant in 7.3% of pairs). Feature component larger.

examples.input: Claim 7: Cross-task transferability of JRN rankings
examples.output: Grade: B (moderate). Same-entity rho=0.82-0.87 (strong), cross-entity rho=0.24 to -0.70 (fails). Per-task recommended.
